purpose: Treat agent work as an auditable process. Project the observable external action trace of each Claude / Codex session into a span-and-trace substrate: timings, sequences, bottlenecks, anti-pattern detections, observed-vs-intended navigation-route compliance, and task-relative path fitness when explicit task-intent metadata exists. This is the observability pipeline for agent work itself — financial accounting for the AI session, not a harness, and explicitly NOT hidden chain-of-thought.

never_parses: message.content[].text prose as raw body, task-intent source, or reasoning reconstruction; bounded status-update classification may emit hashes/rule ids only
tool_result / function_call_output content bodies beyond truncation markers, error flags, and privacy-safe byte/line counts
parses: observable records: timestamp, type, uuid, parentUuid
tool_use / tool_result blocks on Claude (matched by tool_use_id)
function_call / function_call_output records on Codex (matched by call_id)
Read / Edit / Write / Grep / Glob inputs (observable target paths)
tool-result / function-output byte and line counts, plus stdout/stderr byte counts when the provider exposes them as separate fields
retry / throttle / wait markers embedded in result content
strict_boundary.rationale: The observability budget is externalized process only. Raw prose and reasoning are explicitly out of scope — this keeps the substrate a ledger of actions, not a capture of the agent's mind. A status-update classifier may detect bounded repair episode families from assistant-visible updates only when it emits counts, rule ids, timestamps, and text hashes without bodies. Task-intent binding is permitted only from explicit metadata surfaces such as WorkItems, Type B handoff packets, prompt_trace refs, source_refs, operator-provided task labels, or Task Ledger caps; never by mining prompt bodies, assistant prose bodies, or hidden reasoning.

title: Agent Execution Trace Standard

trace_ontology_contract.definition: An agent trace is a source-bound interaction record that preserves observable evidence, authority boundaries, extraction lineage, projection lineage, live/current status, omissions, and receipt-bearing transformations across Type A substrate agents and operator-mediated Type B cognition.

frontend_api_projection.consumer_contract: Frontend trace consumers should render backend-owned primitives and carry trust/status/receipt fields through the view model. They must not create authority by parsing prose summaries.

live_source.definition: A trace source that is still being appended, tailed, polled, sampled, or explicitly captured by the operator with freshness evidence.
live_source.refresh_contract: A live-source claim must be recomputed or rechecked before it is reused as current state in a later task.

raw_current_active_trace.definition: The newest source-backed trace slice for the active interaction.
invalid_claims: latest visible browser tab implies current trace
tab ordinal implies durable session id

source_authority_ladder.contract: May support current-state claims only when the packet carries source ref, freshness/currentness posture, and read boundary.
examples: active Codex rollout JSONL being tailed
active Claude transcript JSONL being tailed
current system-bar clip or trace capture with timestamp and source hash

source_authority_ladder.contract: Durable identity candidate. Use for trace joins and re-openable evidence.
A bounded view over source artifacts. It must carry source refs, generated_at/freshness metadata when available, and omission posture.
Useful for navigation, not durable identity. Promote only when bound to provider/session/source/work/receipt ids.
Can guide Type A action but cannot prove private substrate state until Type A verifies against live/source artifacts.

system_bar_interaction.contract: System-bar copy/capture actions create operator-facing trace packets and prompt packets. They do not automatically ingest into the canonical event store unless a typed ingest path explicitly consumes them.

tab_session_naming.contract: Tab numbers can make active browser state navigable, but they must not be used as durable trace identity without a bound source artifact.

trace_extraction.definition: The bounded act of selecting source facts into typed rows, spans, capsules, packets, or view-model primitives.
must_not_extract: prompt bodies unless explicitly allowed by a private source-bound packet
operator chat content as inferred task intent

type_a_trace.authority_boundary: May support substrate claims when source-backed and within the observable-action boundary.
type_a_trace.definition: Trace from a substrate-native actor with live repo/tool affordances.

type_b_trace.authority_boundary: Advisory until Type A verifies. It must not claim direct access to private files, command results, or generated state unless those were included as source artifacts.
type_b_trace.definition: Operator-mediated web/bridge reasoning trace produced from injected context plus prompt and response.
examples: ChatGPT Web prompt plus trace capsule

trace_projection.definition: A bounded view emitted for human operator use, Type B context packets, APIs, frontend lenses, or cold-agent navigation.
trace_projection.required_posture: Projection, not authority. Preserve source refs and omissions.

type_a_type_b_flow: raw operator cognition, raw trace, screenshot, or active clip
clarity transform or trace capsule extraction
Type B reasoning packet with prompt, context, omissions, and source refs
Type A execution packet with substrate-open targets and assumptions to falsify
Type A live substrate action, validation, and receipts
durable binding to WorkItem, CAP, paper module, standard, API/frontend contract, or generated projection
